mission.title: Install and Configure LiteLLM
mission.description: LiteLLM is a proxy server and SDK for calling over 100 LLM APIs in a unified format, providing features like cost tracking and load balancing. It is ideal for developers looking to integrate multiple AI services seamlessly. Key trade-offs include the complexity of managing multiple API keys and potential latency due to proxying requests.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the necessary command-line interface tools to interact with your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

pip install litellm==1.82.dev1

```

> Install the LiteLLM Python SDK.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in your chosen region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create litellm-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Replace 'us-central1' with your preferred region.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local environment to use the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials litellm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you are authenticated with Google Cloud.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Deploy LiteLLM
steps.description:
This command deploys the LiteLLM application to your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.82.dev1/deploy/kubernetes/litellm-deployment.yaml

```

> This YAML file contains the deployment configuration.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your LiteLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment litellm --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.82.dev1/deploy/kubernetes/litellm-monitoring.yaml

```

> This sets up horizontal pod autoscaling.
> This YAML file sets up monitoring.

uninstall.title: Uninstall LiteLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes the LiteLLM deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data stored in the application.

```bash

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.82.dev1/deploy/kubernetes/litellm-deployment.yaml

gcloud container clusters delete litellm-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This will also remove all orphaned resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade LiteLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This process includes draining the current pods, upgrading the deployment, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.82.dev1/deploy/kubernetes/litellm-deployment.yaml

kubectl get pods

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node you are upgrading.
> This applies the latest deployment configuration.
> Verify that all pods are running and healthy after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image may not exist or the image tag is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and tag in your deployment YAML file.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or the machine type in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be misconfigured or missing environment variables.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and ensure all required environment variables are set.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be running or the endpoint is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify that the LiteLLM service is running and check the service configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new LLM APIs and improved cost tracking features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with all nodes listed as 'Ready'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools installed and configured before proceeding.
